## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On March 31, 1889, the massive, incredibly iconic Eiffel Tower was officially and formally inaugurated in Paris, France. Designed by the brilliant engineer Gustave Eiffel for the massive 1889 Exposition Universelle (World's Fair), the massive, 300-meter iron lattice structure was initially heavily criticized by Parisian artists as a massive eyesore. However, it quickly became an absolute, globally recognized masterpiece of structural engineering and heavily remains the most massive, universally recognized cultural symbol of France.

On March 31, 1917, the United States executed a massive, highly strategic territorial acquisition in the Caribbean. Following a massive $25 million payment in gold coin to the Kingdom of Denmark, the US officially took possession of the Danish West Indies, aggressively renaming them the United States Virgin Islands. This massive geopolitical maneuver was heavily driven by absolute military necessity during World War I, aggressively aiming to establish a massive naval base to protect the highly strategic Panama Canal from German U-boats.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On March 31, 1959, Tenzin Gyatso, the 14th Dalai Lama, successfully completed his massive, incredibly perilous, and highly secretive two-week escape across the brutal Himalayan mountains. Fleeing the massive, aggressive military crackdown by the Chinese People's Liberation Army in Tibet, he officially crossed the massive border into the Tawang region of Arunachal Pradesh, India. Prime Minister Nehru granted him massive, immediate political asylum, deeply infuriating Beijing and fundamentally establishing the massive, enduring Tibetan government-in-exile in Dharamshala.

On March 31, 1865, Anandi Gopal Joshi, a massive, profoundly inspirational, and incredibly determined pioneer in Indian medical history, was born in Maharashtra. Defying massive, intense orthodox societal resistance and severely failing health, she traveled to the United States to aggressively study medicine. She achieved massive historic significance by becoming the absolute, very first Indian woman to obtain a massive degree in Western medicine, serving as a massive, towering inspiration for women's education in the subcontinent.
